title: Introduction to herdr
text: herdr R package estimating greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions feed-related land use livestock production systems. package implements IPCC Tier 2 methodology described 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories provides complete workflow livestock census data environmental impact indicators. Unlike many emission calculators, herdr integrates animal physiology, diet composition, manure management, feed production single modelling framework. package can therefore estimate greenhouse gas emissions agricultural land required sustain livestock diets. Although designed primarily research applications, package can also support teaching, scenario analysis, inventory development, decision-support studies.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: herdr currently supports following production systems: 🐄 Cattle 🐑 Sheep 🐐 Goats 🐖 Swine 🐔 Poultry Additional species may incorporated future releases.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: package follows modular workflow calculations performed sequentially. stage can executed independently using individual functions combined complete assessment using generate_impact_assessment(). modular structure allows users inspect intermediate calculations, validate assumptions, integrate herdr larger modelling workflows.
code: Livestock census         │         ▼  Animal definitions         │         ▼  Diet composition         │         ▼  Energy requirements         │         ▼  Nutrient balance         │         ▼  Greenhouse gas emissions         │         ▼  Feed-related land use         │         ▼  Integrated assessment

title: Introduction to herdr
text: herdr estimates principal greenhouse gas emission sources associated livestock production together land required produce animal feed.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Methane emissions estimated : Enteric fermentation. Manure management. Enteric methane calculated gross energy intake methane conversion factors (Ym), whereas manure methane estimated volatile solids production manure management systems.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Nitrous oxide calculations include: Direct emissions manure management systems. Indirect emissions nitrogen volatilization. Indirect emissions nitrogen leaching runoff.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Land-use calculations link feed consumption crop forage productivity. model combines: feed intake forage yield databases FAOSTAT crop yields international trade information allocation factors estimate land area required produce ingredient consumed animals.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Emission estimates rely complete set intermediate physiological calculations. Depending livestock species, herdr automatically estimates: Gross Energy intake (GE) Digestible Energy (DE) Net Energy requirements Dry Matter Intake (DMI) Nitrogen intake Nitrogen retention Nitrogen excretion Volatile Solids (VS) variables form basis IPCC Tier 2 equations implemented throughout package.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Energy requirements cattle, sheep goats follow equations described IPCC 2019 Refinement. swine poultry, metabolizable energy requirements calculated using methodology published FEDNA adopted Spanish Ministry Agriculture, Fisheries Food (MAPA). package combines energy calculations IPCC emission equations estimate methane nitrous oxide emissions across different livestock systems.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: calculations begin, herdr performs series consistency checks across input datasets. include verification : animal definitions diet assignments body weight information manure management systems duplicated missing combinations biologically implausible values objective identify common input errors environmental indicators calculated.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: Although every calculation can performed directly R, herdr also includes interactive graphical interface. application allows users : edit input tables validate datasets navigate guided workflow run complete assessments visualize summary indicators export results graphical interface uses exactly calculation engine R functions, ensuring identical results regardless model executed.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: package designed collection independent calculation modules. Users may execute individual functions : energy calculations, dry matter intake, methane emissions, nitrogen excretion, land use, run complete assessment using single high-level function. modular architecture simplifies debugging, facilitates sensitivity analyses, allows individual components incorporated external workflows.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: vignette introduces concepts methodology implemented herdr. remaining vignettes provide detailed guidance specific components package: General Workflow — complete project setup execution. Technical Reference — description every input file parameter. Manure Management Guide — supported manure systems classifications. Land Use Methodology — feed allocation land-use calculations. Continue General Workflow vignette prepare first assessment.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: templates must complete run specific analysis. Follow Workflow Guide step--step instructions.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Note origin_country: don’t know feed ingredient comes , leave origin_country column NA. package automatically resolve using international trade data (see fao_trade_matrix.parquet ).

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: needs editing population animal categories unknown, offspring/replacement rates need adjusting specific study.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: files “brain” package. edit unless advanced user — must consult copy exact names required input files.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Consult library find correct ingredient names diet_ingredients.csv. Key columns: Sources: nutritional values come FEDNA Tables (2019). DE_pct values taken Feedipedia. GE_feed_kcal_kg calculated using NRC (1998) Ewan equation: GE(kcal/kg DM)=4140+(56×EE%)+(15×CP%)−(44×ASH%)GE\;(\text{kcal/kg DM}) = 4140 + (56 \times EE\%) + (15 \times CP\%) - (44 \times ASH\%) EE%, CP%, ASH% correspond EE_pct, CP_pct, ASH_pct columns .

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Consult find description need copy livestock_definitions.csv. Key columns: description, coefficient (CaC_a, CfiC_{fi}, etc.), value. matters: contains Tier 2 constants define energy needs maintenance, pregnancy, lactation, well B0B_0 (Maximum Methane Producing Capacity) manure management calculations.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: master list manure management phase. Contains every valid combination manure systems. Key columns: system_base, system_variant, climate_zone, management_months. matters: entry manure_management.csv must match row exactly, model return zero emissions cohort.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: bridge links diet ingredient names agricultural yield databases. Key columns: ingredient, yield_name, allocation (0–1 factor). matters: tells model crop productivity use, much land footprint attributed animal (e.g., grain vs. straw allocation).

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Provisional database forages, supplemented BC3 researchers. Key columns: Area (Country), Item (Crop name), Value (kg DM/ha). matters: provides essential yield data grazing forage-based systems, official FAOSTAT records often incomplete missing.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Direct yield data grains pulses FAOSTAT (2024). Key columns: Area, Item, Year, Value (kg DM/ha). matters: sets international standard calculating land footprint (m²) concentrate feeds commercial crops.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Unlike libraries, file tracked repository, due size (~187 MB). works: automatically downloaded GitHub Releases first time herdr encounters NA origin_country column diets. matters: powers hybrid background allocation engine, using FAO production trade data estimate missing feed origins 70% self-sufficiency rule.

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: Use table know look filling data:

title: Technical Reference: Files & Parameters
text: model case-sensitive tolerate spaces. Always use lowercase underscores (e.g., maize_silage instead Maize Silage).

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: herdr package performs environmental impact assessments based 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines. core model Energy Balance, Gross Energy (GE) intake derived animal’s physiological requirements.

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: NEmNE_m energy required maintain animal’s energy balance without gain loss weight. Formula: NEm=Cfi⋅Weight0.75NE_m = Cf_i \cdot Weight^{0.75}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: NEaNE_a represents energy used grazing, walking, activities. Cattle: NEa=Ca⋅NEmNE_a = C_a \cdot NE_m (Eq. 10.4) Sheep Goats: NEa=Ca⋅WeightNE_a = C_a \cdot Weight (Eq. 10.5)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required tissue deposition (weight gain). Cattle Buffalo: (Eq. 10.6) NEg=22.02⋅(BWC⋅MW)0.75⋅WG1.097NE_{g} = 22.02 \cdot \left( \frac{BW}{C \cdot MW} \right)^{0.75} \cdot WG^{1.097} Sheep Goats: (Eq. 10.7) NEg=WGlamb/kid⋅(+0.5b(BWi+BWf))365NE_g = \frac{WG_{lamb/kid} \cdot (+ 0.5b(BW_i + BW_f))}{365}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required milk production based volume fat content. Cattle: NEl=Milk⋅(1.47+0.40⋅Fat)NE_l = Milk \cdot (1.47 + 0.40 \cdot Fat) (Eq. 10.8) Sheep Goats: NEl=Milk⋅EVmilkNE_l = Milk \cdot EV_{milk} (Eq. 10.9)

what: 5. Net Energy for Work, Wool, and Pregnancy
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Work (NEworkNE_{work}): NEwork=0.10⋅NEm⋅HoursNE_{work} = 0.10 \cdot NE_m \cdot Hours Wool (NEwoolNE_{wool}): NEwool=Prwool⋅EVwool365NE_{wool} = \frac{Pr_{wool} \cdot EV_{wool}}{365} Pregnancy (NEpNE_p): NEp=Cpregnancy⋅NEmNE_p = C_{pregnancy} \cdot NE_m

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Based IPCC Equations 10.14 10.15, derived feed digestibility (DEDE): Maintenance (REMREM): REM=[1.123−(4.092⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.126⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(25.4DE)]REM = \left[ 1.123 - (4.092 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.126 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{25.4}{DE} \right) \right] Growth (REGREG): REG=[1.164−(5.16⋅10−3⋅DE)+(1.308⋅10−5⋅(DE)2)−(37.4DE)]REG = \left[ 1.164 - (5.16 \cdot 10^{-3} \cdot DE) + (1.308 \cdot 10^{-5} \cdot (DE)^2) - \left( \frac{37.4}{DE} \right) \right]

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Gross Energy (GE) total daily energy intake required sustain physiological functions. Formula: GE=(NEm+NEa+NEl+NEwork+NEpREM+NEg+NEwoolREG)DE/100GE = \frac{\left( \frac{NE_m + NE_a + NE_l + NE_{work} + NE_p}{REM} + \frac{NE_g + NE_{wool}}{REG} \right)}{DE/100}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: swine poultry, energy requirements estimated directly metabolizable energy () following FEDNA recommendations, rather IPCC net energy system used ruminants.

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Maintenance requirements calculated : MEmaint=Cfi×WαME_{maint} = C_{fi} \times W^{\alpha} Note: Poultry fixed alpha 0.75.

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: MEgrowth=13.4×Fat×WG+12.0×Protein×WGME_{growth} = 13.4 \times Fat \times WG + 12.0 \times Protein \times WG

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: MEgrowth=12.8×Fat×WG+12.1×Protein×WGME_{growth} = 12.8 \times Fat \times WG + 12.1 \times Protein \times WG

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: laying hens: MEeggs=2×EggmassME_{eggs} = 2 \times Egg_{mass} EggmassEgg_{mass} daily egg mass produced (g/day).

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Conceptus development: MEconceptus=2600×BWbirth×PigletsDaysgest+55.175ME_{conceptus} = \frac{2600 \times BW_{birth} \times Piglets}{Days_{gest}} + 55.175 Note: constant 55.175 represents mammary gland weight gain kcal/day. Metabolizable energy reserve gain: MEreserves=4800×ReservegainDaysgestME_{reserves} = \frac{4800 \times Reserve_{gain}}{Days_{gest}} Total gestation requirement: MEgestation=MEconceptus+MEreservesME_{gestation} = ME_{conceptus} + ME_{reserves}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Milk production: MEmilk=(6.83×WGpiglet−125)×PigletssucklingME_{milk} = (6.83 \times WG_{piglet} - 125) \times Piglets_{suckling} Mobilization body reserves: MEmobilization=−28183DayslactationME_{mobilization} = -\frac{28183}{Days_{lactation}} Total lactation requirement: MElactation=MEmilk+MEmobilizationME_{lactation} = ME_{milk} + ME_{mobilization}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: total daily metabolizable energy requirement : MEtotal=MEmaint+MEgrowth+MEeggs+MEgestation+MElactationME_{total} = ME_{maint} + ME_{growth} + ME_{eggs} + ME_{gestation} + ME_{lactation}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: EF=GE⋅(Ym100)⋅36555.65EF = \frac{GE \cdot \left( \frac{Y_m}{100} \right) \cdot 365}{55.65}

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: ET=∑(P)EF(T,P)⋅(N(T,P)106)E_T = \sum_{(P)} EF_{(T,P)} \cdot \left( \frac{N_{(T,P)}}{10^6} \right)

previous_headings: III. Methane Module > 2. Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)}) > Volatile Solid Excretion Rates (VSVS)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: VS=[GE⋅(1−DE100)+(UE⋅GE)]⋅[1−ASH18.45]VS = \left[ GE \cdot \left(1-\frac{DE}{100}\right) + (UE \cdot GE) \right] \cdot \left[ \frac{1-ASH}{18.45} \right]

previous_headings: III. Methane Module > 2. Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)}) > Volatile Solid Excretion Rates (VSVS)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: VS=DMI⋅(1−MEfeedGEfeed)⋅(1−ASH)VS = DMI \cdot \left( 1-\frac{ME_{feed}}{GE_{feed}} \right) \cdot (1-ASH)

previous_headings: III. Methane Module > 2. Manure Management (CH4(mm)CH_{4(mm)}) > Volatile Solid Excretion Rates (VSVS)
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: VS=DMI⋅(1−DEfeedGEfeed+0.02)⋅(1−ASH)VS = DMI \cdot \left( 1-\frac{DE_{feed}}{GE_{feed}}+0.02 \right) \cdot (1-ASH)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: EF(T)=(VS(T)⋅365)⋅[B0(T)⋅0.67⋅∑S,kMCFS,k100⋅AWMS(T,S,k)]EF_{(T)} = (VS_{(T)} \cdot 365) \cdot \left[ B_{0(T)} \cdot 0.67 \cdot \sum_{S,k} \frac{MCF_{S,k}}{100} \cdot AWMS_{(T,S,k)} \right]

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nitrogen intake calculated differently ruminants monogastric animals. ruminants, nitrogen intake estimated gross energy intake dietary crude protein (IPCC Eq. 10.31/10.32): Nintake=(GE18.45)⋅(CP/1006.25)N_{intake} = \left( \frac{GE}{18.45} \right) \cdot \left( \frac{CP/100}{6.25} \right) swine poultry, nitrogen intake calculated directly dry matter intake: Nintake=DMI⋅(CP/1006.25)N_{intake} = DMI \cdot \left( \frac{CP/100}{6.25} \right)

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nitrogen retention represents fraction nitrogen retained animal products (milk, body tissues, eggs offspring) rather excreted.

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions > 1.2 Nitrogen Retention (NretentionN_{retention})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Milk protein concentration estimated : Milkprotein=1.9+0.4×FatcontentMilk_{protein} = 1.9 + 0.4 \times Fat_{content} lactating growing animals: Nretention=Milk×(Milkprotein/100)6.38+WG(268−7.03×NEgWG)/10006.25N_{retention} = \frac{Milk \times (Milk_{protein}/100)}{6.38} + \frac{WG \left(268-\frac{7.03 \times NE_g}{WG}\right)/1000}{6.25} mature lactating animals, milk term considered. growing animals, growth term considered.

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions > 1.2 Nitrogen Retention (NretentionN_{retention})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Following IPCC Tier 2 methodology, nitrogen retention assumed constant value: Nretention=0.1N_{retention} = 0.1

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions > 1.2 Nitrogen Retention (NretentionN_{retention})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: growing birds: Nretention=(BWfinal−BWinitial)×0.028DaysN_{retention} = \frac{(BW_{final} - BW_{initial}) \times 0.028}{Days} laying hens: Nretention=0.028×WG+0.0185×Eggmass1000N_{retention} = 0.028 \times WG + \frac{0.0185 \times Egg_{mass}}{1000}

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions > 1.2 Nitrogen Retention (NretentionN_{retention})
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: growing pigs: Nretention=(BWfinal−BWinitial)×NgainDaysN_{retention} = \frac{(BW_{final} - BW_{initial}) \times N_{gain}}{Days} breeding sows: Nretention=Ngain,sow+Nweaned365N_{retention} = \frac{N_{gain,sow} + N_{weaned}}{365} Ngain,sow=0.025×FR×SkgN_{gain,sow} = 0.025 \times FR \times S_{kg} Nweaned=0.025×Piglets×FR×(Wweaning−Wbirth)0.98N_{weaned} = \frac{0.025 \times Piglets \times FR \times (W_{weaning} - W_{birth})}{0.98}

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: cattle, swine poultry, annual nitrogen excretion calculated : Nexcreted=(Nintake−Nretention)×365N_{excreted} = (N_{intake} - N_{retention}) \times 365 sheep goats, IPCC assumes fixed nitrogen retention fraction 10%: Nexcreted=Nintake×(1−0.1)×365N_{excreted} = N_{intake} \times (1 - 0.1) \times 365

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 1. Direct Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Direct nitrous oxide emissions manure management calculated following IPCC (2019, Eq. 10.25): N2Odirect=Population×Nexcreted×Allocation×EF3×4428N_2O_{direct} = Population \times N_{excreted} \times Allocation \times EF_3 \times \frac{44}{28}

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 2. Indirect Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Volatilization (Nvolatilization−MMSN_{volatilization-MMS}) (Eq. 10.26) Nvol−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracGasMS]N_{vol-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot N_{ex} \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{GasMS} \right] Leaching Runoff (Nleaching−MMSN_{leaching-MMS}) (Eq. 10.27) Nleach−MMS=∑[(N⋅Nex⋅AWMS)⋅FracLeachMS]N_{leach-MMS} = \sum \left[ (N \cdot N_{ex} \cdot AWMS) \cdot Frac_{LeachMS} \right]

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 2. Indirect Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Volatilization: N2OG(mm)=(Nvol−MMS⋅EF4)⋅4428N_2O_{G(mm)} = (N_{vol-MMS} \cdot EF_4) \cdot \frac{44}{28} Leaching: N2OL(mm)=(Nleach−MMS⋅EF5)⋅4428N_2O_{L(mm)} = (N_{leach-MMS} \cdot EF_5) \cdot \frac{44}{28}

previous_headings: IV. Nitrous Oxide Module > 2. Indirect Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: IPCC (2019). Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Volume 4: AFOLU, Chapter 10.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: vignette explains prepare complete herdr project, creating working directory generating final greenhouse gas land-use assessment. recommended workflow : Install package. Create new R project. Initialize project structure. Prepare input files. Run assessment. Review export results.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Install latest development version directly GitHub.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Create new RStudio project. Working inside .Rproj keeps every project self-contained allows herdr automatically locate required folders input files.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Initialize directory structure running: creates following directories: user_data/: Contains editable CSV input files. Examples/: Contains ready--use example datasets. output/: Stores generated results.

what: 4. Choose how to prepare your data
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: two possible workflows input data.

previous_headings: General Workflow > 4. Choose how to prepare your data
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Launch graphical web interface directly console: interface allows : Edit every input table interactively. Validate datasets real-time. Navigate workflow step--step. Execute model. Download results. option highly recommended first-time users.

previous_headings: General Workflow > 4. Choose how to prepare your data
what: Option B — Edit the CSV files manually
title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Alternatively, can edit CSV templates located inside user_data/ folder using Excel, LibreOffice, spreadsheet software.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: file defines livestock population included assessment. row corresponds one animal cohort. key identifiers : animal_tag region subregion class_flex Together, columns uniquely identify every production group throughout project. population column contains number animals represented row.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Two calculation modes available: Manual population Every animal category entered explicitly. Use automatic_cycle = FALSE populations already known. Automatic herd cycle breeding animals required. package automatically estimates offspring replacement animals using reproductive parameters. Use automatic_cycle = TRUE. (Note: option currently supports predefined livestock categories included package).

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Diet information divided two files.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Defines overall macronutrient distribution diet. four proportions must sum 100%: forage concentrate milk milk_replacer

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Defines specific ingredients composing feed category. Within every category, ingredient percentage must also sum 100%. Dynamic Feed Origin: can specify origin_country ingredient. origin unknown, leave NA. herdr features dynamic background allocation engine automatically estimate origins NA ingredients based FAO trade matrices 70% self-sufficiency rule.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: introducing new custom ingredient, must also update: feed_characteristics.csv (nutritional values) mapping.csv (land-use allocation) forage_yields.csv fao_crop_yields.csv (appropriate)

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: Animal characteristics specified using: livestock_definitions.csv monogastric_definitions.csv livestock_weights.csv files connect animal group physiological parameters, body weights, production periods, diet, IPCC coefficients. Important: Every combination animal_tag, region, subregion, class_flex must exactly match defined livestock census.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: file defines manure managed livestock group. row specifies manure management system, climate, storage conditions, allocation. manure divided among several systems, duplicate row assign corresponding allocation system. every livestock cohort, allocation values must sum 1.0. (Detailed descriptions every supported system available Manure Management Guide vignette).

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: every input file completed, execute main function: function automatically performs data validation, energy calculations, methane estimation, nitrogen nitrous oxide estimation, land-use calculations, result aggregation. ⚠️ Important Data Note: diet contains ingredients unknown origins (NA), first time run function, herdr automatically download required FAO background dataset (approx. 187 MB .parquet format) local environment. one-time process.
code: results <- generate_impact_assessment(   automatic_cycle = FALSE,   farm_country = "Spain",   year = 2022,   saveoutput = TRUE )

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: returned object data frame containing calculated indicators. set saveoutput = TRUE, results also automatically written CSV files output/ folder.

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: execution errors caused inconsistencies among input files. Typical problems include: Missing animal definitions missing diets. Duplicated identifiers. Allocations summing 1 (percentages summing 100). Unknown feed ingredients (mapped properly). Missing body weights. package performs automatic validation calculations begin reports detected problems console help fix .

title: General Workflow: Step-by-Step Guide
text: becoming familiar workflow, consult remaining vignettes detailed information individual components package: Technical Reference Land Use Methodology Manure Management Guide

title: Land Use Methodology
text: addition greenhouse gas emissions, herdr estimates feed-related land use associated livestock production. Land use calculated agricultural area required produce feed consumed livestock population. model combines feed intake, crop forage productivity, allocation factors, international trade information estimate total land requirement diet. Results expressed square metres (m²) calculated dry matter (DM) basis.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Land use calculated independently every feed ingredient using three sequential steps.

what: Step 1. Land requirement per kilogram of feed
title: Land Use Methodology
text: inverse crop yield represents land area required produce one kilogram dry matter. LandPerKgi=1YieldiLandPerKg_i = \frac{1}{Yield_i} Yield expressed kilograms dry matter per hectare (kg DM/ha).

title: Land Use Methodology
text: crops generate multiple products (example grain straw). avoid assigning entire land area single product, land requirement multiplied allocation factor. AdjustedLandPerKgi=1Yieldi×AllocationiAdjustedLandPerKg_i = \frac{1}{Yield_i} \times Allocation_i allocation factor ranges 0 1 defined mapping.csv.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Annual feed consumption multiplied adjusted land requirement converted hectares square metres. LandUsei=AnnualConsumptioni×(1Yieldi×Allocationi)×10000LandUse_i = AnnualConsumption_i \times \left( \frac{1}{Yield_i} \times Allocation_i \right) \times 10\,000 AnnualConsumption annual dry matter intake (kg DM/year), Yield crop productivity (kg DM/ha), Allocation economic allocation factor, 10,000 converts hectares square metres. total land use reported herdr obtained summing contribution every ingredient included diet.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: herdr combines two complementary yield databases.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Crop productivity obtained official FAOSTAT statistics. data include major feed crops cereals, oilseeds legumes expressed country-level average yields.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Official global statistics forage productivity often unavailable. reason, herdr includes curated forage yield database compiled multiple literature sources technical reports provide broad geographical coverage. Users performing high-resolution regional studies encouraged replace values local data whenever available.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Land-use calculations rely file mapping.csv, links feed ingredients corresponding yield database. columns following meaning: ingredient — ingredient name used diet_ingredients.csv; yield_name — corresponding crop forage name yield databases; allocation — proportion land attributed ingredient. Every ingredient included diet must corresponding entry mapping.csv.

what: Feed origin and dynamic background allocation
title: Land Use Methodology
text: Land-use calculations require user specify country farm located assessment year. origin feed ingredients dictates crop yields applied. Users can explicitly define origin ingredient input files. However, exact origin unknown (left NA), herdr employs dynamic background allocation engine. hybrid engine utilizes FAO production trade data calculate missing feed origins based 70% self-sufficiency rule. estimates proportion ingredient produced domestically versus proportion imported, automatically distributing land footprint across respective trade partner countries. ⚠️ Important Data Note: perform dynamic calculations without bloating package size, herdr relies external high-resolution FAO trade matrix. first time run calculation requires background origin allocation, package automatically download fao_trade_matrix.parquet file (approx. 187 MB) local environment.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Several assumptions considered interpreting land-use estimates. calculations performed dry matter (DM) basis. Crop yields represent country-average productivity rather farm-specific values. Forage yields compiled multiple published sources may represent local management conditions. Allocation factors reflect accounting approach adopted user. background allocation engine relies 70% self-sufficiency assumption together historical FAO trade matrices, representing macro-economic trade flows rather farm-level supply chains. Results interpreted estimates land required produce feed, direct measurements occupied agricultural land.

title: Land Use Methodology
text: Land use can estimated independently using standalone function: Alternatively, land-use calculations performed automatically running complete assessment generate_impact_assessment().

code: # Install if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("remotes") } remotes::install_github("JuanCBM99/herdr")  library(herdr)  # Initialize project herdr_init()  # Load example data file.copy(list.files("Examples/Level1_Spain_Dairy_Cattle_2015", full.names = TRUE), "user_data")  # Run model results <- generate_impact_assessment(automatic_cycle = FALSE, farm_country = "Spain", year = 2015)
